text.title: GraphRAG — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of `graph-rag` must enforce. Verifiable at review time; failures gate the artifact.

rule.statement: Adopt GraphRAG only if multi-hop or global questions are ≥20% of the workload.

rationale.source: Microsoft GraphRAG paper 2024; Neo4j RAG benchmarks

rule.rationale: Below 20% the graph cost (build + maintain + query latency) exceeds the benefit.

rule.statement: Define entity-type + relation-type schema BEFORE extracting; never let extraction invent types.
rule.rationale: Open-schema extraction yields fragmented, un-mergeable graphs.

rule.statement: Pin the extraction prompt + model + schema version; re-extract on any change.
rule.rationale: Drift in prompt or model yields incompatible graph slices.

rule.statement: Run Leiden or Louvain community detection on the entity graph; cache communities.
rule.rationale: Communities partition the graph into theme-clusters that ground global queries.

rule.statement: Build summaries per community at ≥2 levels of granularity (community + super-community).
rule.rationale: Hierarchical summaries answer global questions without scanning the whole graph.

rule.statement: Classify each query as local (entity-subgraph), global (community-summary), or hybrid; route accordingly.
rule.rationale: Routing keeps local queries fast (vector RAG path) and reserves graph traversal for the cases that need it.
